title: Intro to Tailwind CSS Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of the Tailwind CSS Framework role, utility-first design, JIT compiler, and customization.

questions.question: Which and/or what is Tailwind's primary role in a web application?
options: A utility-first CSS framework that provides low-level helper classes for building custom UIs directly in HTML.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.
To replace the standard HTML input elements.

questions.question: Does Tailwind CSS provide highly opinionated component classes like .btn or .card?
options: No, it provides low-level utilities (like bg-blue-500, p-4) that you combine to build your own components.

questions.question: What is the key benefit of Tailwind's 'Just-In-Time' (JIT) compiler?
options: It makes the page load faster.
It only generates the exact CSS you use on each page, keeping the final bundle size small.

questions.question: Which configuration file is used to customize Tailwind's default theme (colors, fonts, etc.)?
Does Tailwind CSS allow you to build truly unique and custom interfaces with minimal context-switching?
options: Yes, as it provides a comprehensive set of built-in utility classes for almost any style.
